label: PASS
rationale: Range1 has WriteLockEnabled true and LockOnReset {Power Cycle}; a PowerCycle sets the stored ReadLocked and WriteLocked cells to true, so the returned columns 6-9 are consistent.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range1's LockOnReset contains Power Cycle, so the PowerCycle must set WriteLocked true. Returning WriteLocked false contradicts the reset rule.

label: PASS
rationale: Range2 has an empty LockOnReset set; reset events do not change ReadLocked or WriteLocked, so the previously unlocked stored cells remain false.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range2's empty LockOnReset means PowerCycle does not change WriteLocked. Returning WriteLocked true after it was set false is unsupported.

label: FAIL
rationale: After PowerCycle, Range1 has WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked true, so writes to LBAs 100-107 must not be allowed. A successful host write is not protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Range2 remains WriteLocked false because its LockOnReset is empty; with WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked false, writes to LBAs 120-127 are allowed.

label: FAIL
rationale: The target LBA range is unlocked for writes, so an INVALID_PARAMETER write result is not supported by the provided locking evidence or trajectory.

label: PASS
rationale: Level 0 Locked is one when any range has an enabled lock whose locked cell is true. Range1 has WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked true after PowerCycle.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because Range1 is write-lock enabled and write locked, the Level 0 Locked bit must be one. Returning Locked zero is inconsistent with the specified aggregate rule.

label: PASS
rationale: After disabling WriteLockEnabled on the only locked range, its WriteLocked cell is ignored for locking. The other range is not locked, so Level 0 Locked is zero.

label: FAIL
rationale: With Range1's WriteLockEnabled set false and ReadLockEnabled already false, no enabled locked range remains. Level 0 Locked one is therefore unsupported.

label: PASS
rationale: A Programmatic reset applies only to rows whose LockOnReset contains Programmatic. Range1 contains only Power Cycle, so its stored locked cells remain false.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range1 does not include Programmatic in LockOnReset, so TPerReset must not set its WriteLocked cell true. The returned WriteLocked true is unsupported.

label: PASS
rationale: Range2 has LockOnReset {Power Cycle, Programmatic}; TPerReset is Programmatic, so both stored ReadLocked and WriteLocked cells are set true.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because Range2's LockOnReset includes Programmatic, TPerReset sets WriteLocked true. Returning WriteLocked false contradicts the reset behavior.

label: PASS
rationale: Range1 is power-only, so Programmatic reset leaves its WriteLocked cell false. With WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked false, the host write is allowed.

label: FAIL
rationale: The power-only Range1 remains write-unlocked across Programmatic reset, so rejecting a write to its LBAs is not supported by the trajectory.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range2 includes Programmatic in LockOnReset and has WriteLockEnabled true, so TPerReset makes writes to LBAs 120-127 disallowed. A successful write is non-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Manual clearing of WriteLocked is later overridden by PowerCycle because Range1's LockOnReset contains Power Cycle; both stored locked cells are true afterward.

label: FAIL
rationale: PowerCycle matches Range1's LockOnReset and sets WriteLocked true even after manual unlock. A returned WriteLocked false is inconsistent.

label: PASS
rationale: Range2's LockOnReset contains Power Cycle as well as Programmatic; a PowerCycle relocks it, so both stored ReadLocked and WriteLocked are true.

label: FAIL
rationale: Range2 includes Power Cycle in LockOnReset, so PowerCycle must set WriteLocked true. Returning WriteLocked false is unsupported.
